text: Discover Weekly used to be magic. Now every Monday it just gives me artists I already saved. Where's the discovery?
author: anon_001

text: I listen to indie folk on weekends and lofi while working. Spotify keeps mixing them up in Daily Mix. I want context-aware recommendations, not just genre buckets.
author: anon_002

text: Smart Shuffle is supposed to introduce new songs but it picks tracks too similar to what's in my playlist. Feels like the algorithm is afraid to suggest anything actually new.
author: anon_003

text: As a Tamil music listener, Spotify's recommendations are stuck on three superstar composers. There are hundreds of indie Tamil artists it never surfaces. Regional discovery is broken.
author: anon_004

text: Release Radar gives me 30 songs every Friday but 25 of them are from artists I already follow. The 'new' part is the lie.
author: anon_005

text: I'm a free user. Forced shuffle and skip limits mean I can't even explore a new album properly. By the time I hear a track I might like, I've used my skips.
author: anon_006

text: Spotify DJ is fun for two minutes then it loops back to my top 50 artists. It's the same algorithm with a voice on top, not real discovery.
author: anon_007

text: I want to discover music to study to, but every playlist labelled 'focus' is the same lo-fi loop. Where are the global classical, ambient electronic, neoclassical options?
author: anon_008

text: Why can't I tell Spotify 'recommend things I haven't heard before'? It needs a discovery intensity slider. Right now everything is at safety level 10.
author: anon_009

text: Liked Songs becomes a black hole. Once I hit Like on something, my Daily Mixes are dominated by that vibe for weeks. The algorithm overcorrects on every signal.
author: anon_010

text: I switched from Apple Music expecting better recs. Two weeks in, Spotify is recommending the same Drake, Weeknd, Taylor Swift loop my friends say theirs does. Not personalized at all.
author: anon_011

text: Daylist is a great idea but the labels are wackier than the music. The actual tracks are still my usual stuff with different cover art.
author: anon_012

text: Bhojpuri folk is huge in north India. Spotify's Hindi recommendations don't touch it. The catalogue is there but the algorithm pretends it isn't.
author: anon_013

text: I've been a heavy user for 8 years. My On Repeat feels like a museum. I keep coming back to songs from 2018 because nothing new sticks.
author: anon_014

text: Spotify Blend is nice but it's discovery through other people only. I want algorithmic blend with multiple moods I pick myself.
author: anon_015

text: The search-and-play loop is killing exploration. I think of an artist, search, play, queue similar — I never browse anymore. The home tab feels stale.
author: anon_016

text: Autoplay after a playlist used to introduce me to new artists. Now it autoplays songs from my Liked Songs. They literally replaced discovery with familiarity.
author: anon_017

text: I love jazz but Spotify only shows me the famous standards. There's a century of jazz history and the algorithm acts like nothing exists past Miles Davis greatest hits.
author: anon_018

text: Wrapped told me I listened to the same 20 artists all year. I wasn't shocked. I was sad. Spotify is showing me my own filter bubble back to me like it's an achievement.
author: anon_019

text: Why is there no 'try a new genre this week' mode? Even YouTube has the Explore tab. Spotify's discovery is buried inside playlists.
author: anon_020

text: I'm a classical music fan. Niche Mixes finally gave me 'baroque chamber' but it's the same 30 pieces in every variation. Repetition repackaged.
author: anon_021

text: When I'm cooking I want background music I don't have to think about. When I'm running I want hype. Spotify can't read context. Apple's focus modes know more about my state than my $10/mo music app.
author: anon_022

text: I asked the DJ to play something Indian classical. It played Diljit Dosanjh. Cool artist, wrong category. The 'AI' clearly doesn't understand music families.
author: anon_023

text: I want a button that says 'I love this artist, now find me 5 artists in the same scene I've never heard'. Not similar genre. Same scene. The algorithm doesn't grasp scenes.
author: anon_024

text: I'm 19 and most of my playlist is from before I was born. I want what's coming up now in 2026 — emerging artists, not viral TikTok loops. Where do I find that on Spotify?
author: anon_025

text: Made For You section is just six Daily Mixes that all sound the same plus Discover Weekly and Release Radar. That's it. That's the whole discovery experience for paying users.
author: anon_026

text: Tried the Enhance feature on my chill playlist. It added more songs that all sound the same. Enhance should mean variety, not amplification.
author: anon_027

text: The whole point of paying for Premium was supposed to be access plus discovery. Access is great. Discovery is the same algorithm the free tier gets, just without ads.
author: anon_028

text: I miss the old Friend Activity sidebar on mobile. Seeing what friends actually listened to was the best discovery feature Spotify ever had. They killed it for some reason.
author: anon_029

text: I'm a Malayali in Bangalore. I want to discover new Tamil and Telugu music too, not just Malayalam. Spotify's regional silos are too tight. I'm South Indian, not just one language.
author: anon_030

text: Why does the queue auto-fill with my top tracks? I queue one new song from a friend and Spotify pads it with my own favourites. Let me try the new song without going back to safety.
author: anon_031

text: I keep ending up in 'sad indie' even though I listen to it for one hour a week. The algorithm weights vibes weirdly. A small input takes over my entire profile.
author: anon_032

text: Daily Mix 1 and Daily Mix 2 share 60% of the same songs. Daily Mix 4-6 are basically my Liked Songs in different orders. 'Made for you' is 'made from you' on repeat.
author: anon_033

text: I want recommendations based on mood I tell Spotify, not the mood it infers from yesterday's listening. Sometimes Monday isn't like Sunday.
author: anon_034

text: I follow 200+ artists. Release Radar only surfaces from the same 20. The other 180 might as well not be on the platform for me.
author: anon_035

text: There's no way to say 'I want music in Spanish but I don't speak Spanish, recommend me hits'. It defaults to my locale way too aggressively.
author: anon_036

text: When I skip a song, the algorithm should learn. Instead it recommends the same artist's other tracks 30 minutes later. Skips are not negative signals here?
author: anon_037

text: I want to know WHY a song was recommended to me. 'Because you liked X' would help me trust the algorithm. The black box makes me distrust recommendations.
author: anon_038

text: Music discovery should feel like a conversation. Apple's Shazam, YouTube's algorithm, even SoundCloud has better serendipity. Spotify's recommendation has plateaued.
author: anon_039

text: I'm 45 and my Daily Mixes are full of 90s alt rock. I lived through it. I want to discover music made by 20-year-olds in 2026, just maybe not TikTok hits. There's no setting for that.
author: anon_040

text: I have a long commute. I'd love a 30-minute 'one new artist deep-dive' format. Spotify gives me playlists, not curated journeys.
author: anon_041

text: Spotify Wrapped is great marketing. The other 364 days a year discovery is on autopilot. We need 'Mini-Wrapped' moments that nudge me out of my bubble monthly.
author: anon_042

text: Stuck in a metal loop for 3 years. Tried liking a country song to break out. Now my Daily Mix is metal AND country. Algorithm doesn't replace tastes, it stacks them.
author: anon_043

text: Why isn't there a 'fresh ears mode' that ignores my history for a session and recommends from a curated pool? Sometimes I want to escape myself.
author: anon_044

text: Discovery on Spotify works for English pop and US/UK indie. Outside that, it's basically a static catalogue. Korean indie, Brazilian jazz, Afrobeats deep cuts — all missing from my recs.
author: anon_045

text: I want Spotify to recommend music by *vibe* like 'sounds like a rainy 1970s Tokyo' not by genre tags. That's what AI is for, right?
author: anon_046

text: Free tier locks me into shuffle on most playlists. Discovery means hearing what I want WHEN I want, not Spotify's random pick. Subscription is a discovery gate.
author: anon_047

text: On Repeat playlist is depressing. It tells me I'm in a loop and offers no escape ladder. Why isn't there a 'break the loop' button right there?
author: anon_048

text: I want Spotify to recommend music my favourite artists love but didn't make. Like influences and contemporaries. That's actual taste-graph discovery.
author: anon_049

text: The home tab shows me 6 'Made For You' playlists, 4 of which I never click. Spotify won't kill them because the metrics say I open the home tab. Vanity engagement.
author: anon_050

text: I'd pay extra for a 'discovery concierge' — an AI that knows my whole library, my friends' libraries, and curates 1 song a day I've never heard with context. Spotify won't ship that because Premium is one tier.
author: anon_051

text: Casual user here. I open Spotify maybe 3 times a week. The algorithm needs 30 minutes daily to update. Casual users get worse and worse recs over time, not better.
author: anon_052

text: When my partner uses my account at the gym, my Discover Weekly is wrecked for a month. Profile contamination is a thing. Why no per-context profile?
author: anon_053

text: Niche Mixes is the best thing Spotify shipped in 2 years. But there are only 12 of mine and the songs in each are static for weeks. Discovery within a niche should be daily.
author: anon_054

text: Asked DJ to 'play me something different'. It played me an artist I've listened to 400 times. 'Different' should mean unknown, not unplayed-this-week.
author: anon_055

text: I'm a music student. I want Spotify to teach me music history through my listening. There's no path from 'songs I like' to 'roots of why I like them'. Discovery is shallow.
author: anon_056

text: Bollywood lover. Spotify pushes the same 50 90s tracks because my history skews 90s. New independent Hindi artists exist on the platform but never reach me.
author: anon_057

text: Daylist sometimes nails it for an hour, then resets to my Liked Songs. The discovery window inside the app is one hour wide. Then I'm back home.
author: anon_058

text: I want a discovery slider in the app: comfort ←→ adventurous. Today everything sits at comfort=10. Let me dial it.
author: anon_059

text: Punjabi music is the most listened genre in India. Spotify recommends Diljit and Sidhu on loop. There are 500 active Punjabi artists. The catalog exists. The exposure doesn't.
author: anon_060

text: I keep clicking 'thumbs down' on songs I don't like. The algorithm thanks me and recommends the same artist a week later. Negative signals are noise to Spotify.
author: anon_061

text: Discover Weekly has had the same 'flavor' for 4 years. Like Spotify thinks I'm the same person I was when I was 23. Algorithm doesn't age with me.
author: anon_062

text: I want to discover music my dad listened to. There's no way to say 'recommend music from the 1970s Calcutta scene' or 'recommend what a 60-year-old Bengali would love'.
author: anon_063

text: The autoplay queue after a single is terrible. Plays a song from the artist's own album next. Same artist 5 in a row. That's not discovery, that's a digital cassette.
author: anon_064

text: I'm bilingual Tamil-English. Spotify forces me to pick one for my locale. My discovery is split and neither half is good. Multilingual users are second-class.
author: anon_065

text: When I make a playlist, the 'Enhance' button suggests songs already in another of my playlists. So the algorithm DOES know my whole library — it just won't venture outside it.
author: anon_066

text: Late-night discovery used to be a vibe. Now I open Spotify at 11pm and it plays me what I played at 11am. Time-of-day intelligence is wasted.
author: anon_067

text: I want Spotify to occasionally ignore me. Surprise me with one song a day that breaks my pattern. Right now everything is pattern reinforcement.
author: anon_068

text: Spotify Connect is great. But Connect into my friend's session and discover what THEY discovered yesterday? That feature doesn't exist. Social discovery is broken.
author: anon_069

text: I have ADHD and need novelty. Spotify's algorithm is built for comfort listeners. There should be an attention-driven mode that constantly cycles new artists.
author: anon_070

text: Free tier on mobile is brutal for discovery. I can't pick the song to test a new artist. The 6 skips per hour kill exploration immediately.
author: anon_071

text: I want a tab called 'Today's frontier' — 10 songs I've never heard, refreshed daily, no Liked Songs included. Why don't I have that?
author: anon_072

text: Stuck listening to the same K-Pop because Spotify's 'K-Pop Mix' is just BTS, Blackpink, Twice in rotation. Korean indie, hip-hop, R&B all exist. Spotify doesn't show them.
author: anon_073

text: I want to discover live versions, demos, covers, alternates of songs I love. Spotify treats them as separate releases buried in artist pages. Discovery within an artist's catalog is broken too.
author: anon_074

text: I'm a casual Indian listener. I open Spotify on commute, get the same 20 Hindi songs in 'Top Hits India'. My discovery loop is shorter than the actual playlist.
author: anon_075

text: I want music discovery to be social by default. What's trending in my college, my city, my workplace — granular trending. Top Charts India is too broad for any of those.
author: anon_076

text: Blend with strangers, not just friends, would be powerful. 'Blend with someone in Tokyo who likes Tame Impala' — that's discovery. Spotify won't ship it because the social graph is too small.
author: anon_077

text: DJ feature voice is annoying after 5 minutes. Skipped intros, music underneath was generic. Voice over without unique recs is just marketing.
author: anon_078

text: I want Spotify to suggest the album, not the algorithmically-curated single. Album-first discovery is dying because of how the app is built around individual tracks.
author: anon_079

text: I follow 12 podcasts and 200 artists. Home tab leads with podcasts even though I only listen on Sundays. Discovery surface area for music is shrinking.
author: anon_080

text: Premium Family discovery is broken. Each profile gets isolated recs even though we share taste. Why can't profiles cross-pollinate on opt-in?
author: anon_081

text: Genre walls. Spotify thinks if I like prog rock I must hate hip-hop. Reality: I love both. Algorithm is binary about tastes, humans are not.
author: anon_082

text: I move countries every 6 months for work. My recommendations get whiplash. There's no 'I'm a global listener, ignore my IP' setting.
author: anon_083

text: Spotify's AI Playlist (the prompt-driven one) is hit or miss. Half the time it returns songs I've heard 100 times. The other half it picks tangentially related stuff. No middle ground.
author: anon_084

text: Discovery is broken for new accounts too. I made a fresh account, listened to 20 jazz tracks. Discover Weekly week 1 was country pop. The cold-start problem is real.
author: anon_085

text: Why is there no 'rabbit hole' mode? Pick an artist, get 30 minutes of their influences, then 30 of their imitators, then their contemporaries. A guided journey, not a playlist.
author: anon_086

text: I want to filter Discover Weekly by 'not English'. Or 'released in last 6 months'. Or 'no streams above 1M'. The discovery layer has zero filters. Black box only.
author: anon_087

text: Indian women in tech, 20s, Premium subscriber. My recs are 80% female pop and 20% bollywood. I asked for none of that. The demographic profiling is louder than my actual taste.
author: anon_088

text: I'd love a 'discover by lyric theme' search — songs about leaving home, songs about Mondays, songs about specific emotions. Lyrical discovery doesn't exist.
author: anon_089

text: When I save a song, it should go into a context-aware bucket, not just 'Liked Songs'. Right now my morning meditation tracks live next to my Friday party music in one pile.
author: anon_090

text: Spotify Wrapped reveals filter bubble depth but offers no remedy. Imagine if Wrapped came with a 12-month discovery roadmap. Right now it's just a marketing artefact.
author: anon_091

text: Family plan, my 14yo cousin uses my account once and DJ starts playing K-Pop for a week. The model recovers slowly. Why no 'this listen was not me' button?
author: anon_092

text: I want context-aware playlists generated on the fly — 'I'm walking home in monsoon Mumbai, give me 30 minutes'. That's where AI should shine. Spotify's AI Playlist isn't ambient-aware.
author: anon_093

text: There's no 'unlike everything from past month' lever. Tastes evolve. Spotify clings to old data. Forgetting is a feature humans need from algorithms.
author: anon_094

text: Old-school user, 50yo. Spotify won't recommend me anything younger than 1990. I want to like Phoebe Bridgers, I just don't know about her. The platform won't introduce us.
author: anon_095

text: Daylist's whimsical names ('feral catstep monday morning') feel like creative writing exercises on top of the same audio. Cute UI, lazy algorithm.
author: anon_096

text: I want to discover music attached to film/series I love. Spotify has soundtrack playlists but they don't connect to my recommendations. Disconnect between cultural objects and discovery.
author: anon_097

text: When I add a song to a playlist, that signal should not pollute my personal recommendations. I'm DJing for friends, not myself. Spotify can't tell the difference.
author: anon_098

text: I keep returning to the same 40 tracks not because I love them but because they're the path of least resistance in the app. Discovery friction is too high, repeat-listen friction is zero.
author: anon_099

text: Spotify won the streaming wars. Now they've stopped innovating on discovery. The 'World's best recommendation system' is coasting on its 2018 reputation while users quietly loop the same 100 songs.
author: anon_100

extra._video_title: We Cracked The Spotify Algorithm... Again

text: Fav tidbits from this: if you hit a 20 popularity score, you start getting an algorithmic push on release radar. If you hit about a 30, you start getting it on discover weekly as well. To get a popularity for a 20 on average about 3800 streams. And that 3,800 will get you a release radar push. You needed 2,500 before. So you need about 50% More Streams to get the same popularity index as before now. It’s gotten harder to hit to increase popularity scores, which makes it harder to get on algorithmic playlist. Market saturation could affect this too. The most popular song is the more streams you’re gonna get from Radio. The more you market, the more they’re gonna give you. The more popular you are, the more you get for free from them. But makes sense, because more popularity, the more data they have and the more they can recommend your music. Streams are the only things that matter. Saves, playlist adds, release date, etc- don’t really matter. They just benefit because they lead to more streams. Streams are a side effect of all the other things. It takes about 13,000 streams to get a discover weekly push.
author: @Kookyverse

text: Thank you for this, Andrew. We have made a similar analysis with 175k songs we're extending it to 1.5mio songs, but it just needs time. However, one of the problems we have faced with the analysis, that there's one major driver that explains (r2) 95% of the predictiable success of a song, and that's this factor: major label and/or it's subsidiaries. When removing that factor our R-squared is close to 65%. So, honestly, I'm surprised you come up with 92% using only a fraction of samples to test with. Anyway, wishing you great success improving your model, curious to see and hear more!
